Replace rubyzip ZIP writing with zip_kit for streaming exports (#179)

* Replace rubyzip ZIP writing with zip_kit for streaming exports

OBZ/ZIP exports buffered entire archives in memory via rubyzip, causing
500MB+ memory spikes on the 2GB staging server. zip_kit writes ZIPs in a
streaming, append-only fashion with flat ~128KB memory overhead.

- Add zip_kit ~> 6.3 alongside rubyzip (different namespaces, no conflict)
- Monkey-patch OBF::Utils.build_zip to use ZipKit::Streamer (kill-switch
  via OBF_ZIPKIT_PATCH=0). OBF::Utils.load_zip stays on rubyzip for reading.
- Stream inner OBZ/PDF files from disk via IO.copy_stream instead of
  loading entire files into memory with file.read
- Add ensure blocks to export_user, export_logs, and generate_zip to
  guarantee tempfile cleanup on failure (prevents disk exhaustion)

Fix StarRocks FE/BE/CN CPU limits to prevent LimitRange conflict

The cluster has a LimitRange that defaults CPU limit to 8 cores.
Setting CN (and BE) cpu_request to 16000m while omitting an explicit
cpu_limit caused Kubernetes to reject the StatefulSet because the
request exceeded the default limit.

Now all three specs (FE, BE, CN) explicitly set limits.cpu equal to
their cpu_request by default, overridable via a cpu_limit stack config
key.  This prevents any cluster LimitRange from silently capping CPU
below what we request.

* Use AllDirectories in DetectTestFramework to find nested csproj files

* Use Name.LocalName for XML queries in DetectTestFramework

Descendants('ElementName') won't match elements in .csproj files that

resilient to both namespaced and non-namespaced project files.

refactor!: replace Agent/Orchestrator with Implementor, Evaluator, Validator interfaces

Introduce three specialized interfaces extending Agent<TContext, TResult>:
- Implementor<TContext>: produces code changes, returns ImplementorResult
- Evaluator<TContext>: scores changes, returns EvaluatorResult
- Validator<TContext>: gate decisions via rule(): Rule<TContext>, returns GatekeeperResult

All agents are now class instances implementing one of the three interfaces
(or base Agent for SynthesizerAgent). Zero free agent functions remain.

Key changes:
- Rule<A> is now fully async: (ctx: A) => Promise<Tri>
- Gate.lift() converts sync lambdas to async rules
- Gate combinators (and/or/not) await internally
- New llmAsJudge<A>() factory creates LLM-backed Validators
- Orchestrator interface removed; orchestrators implement their role

BREAKING CHANGE: Orchestrator interface removed. Rule<A> signature changed
from sync to async. All runXxx() free functions replaced by class instances.

perf: replace sync fs calls with async in completion.ts

Convert all synchronous file system operations to async equivalents
to prevent blocking the LSP server event loop:

- fs.readdirSync() → fs.promises.readdir() in getHrefFileCompletions()
- fs.readFileSync() → fs.promises.readFile() in getHrefFragmentCompletions()
  and getKeyrefCompletions()
- fs.existsSync() → try/catch around async readFile (ENOENT handled)
- Import changed from 'fs' to 'fs/promises'
- Added MAX_DIR_ENTRIES (200) cap to prevent huge directory listings
  from degrading completion performance

All three functions were already called from async contexts, so the
return type changes (→ Promise<CompletionItem[]>) propagate cleanly.
824 server tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: preserve StreamDetails.data in cache (fixes NoneType streaming error)

StreamDetails.data has serialize='omit' in music_assistant_models,
so to_dict() strips it. On cache hit the deserialized object had
data=None, causing TypeError in get_audio_stream when checking
'decryption_key' not in None.

Now manually include data in the cached dict so URL and decryption
key survive the round-trip.

feat: DirectoryTransport base class (#37)

Implements DirectoryTransport — the base transport for filesystem I/O at any path.
WorktreeTransport will extend this class for git worktree operations.

Full TeamTransport interface implementation:
- readFile, writeFile, exists, stat (filesystem I/O)
- readStatus (status.json with Zod validation)
- readInboxSignals, writeInboxSignal, readOutboxSignals (signal protocol)
- readLearningLog, appendLearning (learning log JSONL)
- listSignals (with type/since/from filtering)
- watchSignals (fs.watch-based real-time updates)
- workspaceExists, getLocation (workspace management)
- listFiles (recursive directory listing)
- bootstrap (workspace initialization)

Engineering standards:
- Strict TypeScript, zero any types
- Comprehensive JSDoc on all public methods
- Zod validation for all reads (status.json, signals, learning log)
- Async fs/promises operations throughout
- Signal filename format: {ISO-timestamp}-{type}-{subject-slug}.json
- Detailed error messages with context
